page: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge is a specialized instance family designed for deep learning inference workloads, featuring Qualcomm inference accelerators. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 virtual CPUs and 48 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 35 MB of L3 cache and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 with Vector Neural Network Instructions for accelerated vector operations.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This large memory footprint is backed by strong memory bandwidth, with cached read-only small block performance reaching 4504.08 GB/sec and uncached large block read performance at 220.46 GB/sec. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is robust, featuring a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 81310.77 and a PassMark CPU score of 55760.63. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1370.79 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression rate. Conversely, the instance excels in parallelized tasks, demonstrating high throughput in Redis SET operations at 75.32 million operations per second and static web serving at 45.89 GB/sec.

Given its hardware profile, the dl2q.24xlarge is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive parallel processing and deep learning inference workloads that leverage its dedicated Qualcomm accelerators without requiring expensive general-purpose GPUs. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge is an x86_64 deep learning inference instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 2.5 GHz clock speed. It features 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, but includes no local storage. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. While single-core performance is modest, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are strong, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 81310.77 and Redis SET operations reaching 75.32 million operations per second. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for parallelized workloads, database operations, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive deep learning inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge is a deep learning inference instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, 100 Gbps network bandwidth, and Intel Xeon processors optimized for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services dl2q.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps bandwidth, optimized for parallel deep learning inference.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, memory-dense inference platform featuring ninety-six virtual cores and specialized vector acceleration for highly parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
Optimized for deep learning inference, Redis caching, and database operations
